hook.text: If you're needing to use really strong pain killers, get help.
hook.emotional_mechanism_raw: Alarm created by suggesting that requiring strong painkillers for periods is abnormal and necessitates seeking help.
hook.target_audience_raw: Individuals experiencing severe menstrual pain who may not recognize it as a symptom of endometriosis

main_claim.text: Painful periods are often one of the first and most well-known symptoms of endometriosis, and it can progress if untreated.

supporting_explanation.summary: The video explains that endometriosis can start as early as age 11-12, impacting education, social life, and leading to labeling. It calls for awareness in schools and among parents and teachers.

funnel_rationale: Video primarily educates about endometriosis symptoms and advocates for broader awareness, typical of top-of-funnel content. While it includes a booking CTA, the main message is educational.

cta.wording: go to the link in our bio to book a consultation with Liz Bruen
cta.requested_action_raw: Navigate to link in bio and book a consultation with the endometriosis nurse
cta.value_exchange_raw: Access to a specialist endometriosis nurse for a private virtual consultation

format_raw: Narrated educational video with on-screen text overlays
